Minions (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $336,045,770Details
International Box Office $821,225,989
Worldwide Box Office $1,157,271,759
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $60,291,890
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $65,506,499
Total $125,798,389
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Starting as single-celled yellow organisms, Minions evolve through the ages, perpetually serving the most despicable of masters. Continuously unsuccessful at keeping these masters-from T. rex to Napoleon-the Minions find themselves without someone to serve and fall into a deep depression. But one Minion named Kevin has a plan, and he-alongside teenage rebel Stuart and lovable little Bob-ventures out into the world to find a new evil boss for his brethren to follow. The trio embarks upon a thrilling journey that ultimately leads them to their next potential master, Scarlet Overkill, the world's first-ever female super-villain. They travel from frigid Antarctica to 1960s New York City, ending in mod London, where they must face their biggest challenge to date: saving all of Minionkind...from annihilation.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$115,718,405 (34.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.90 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$74,000,000 (worldwide box office is 15.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,301 opening theaters/4,311 max. theaters, 7.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $476,762,449

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 10th, 2015 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: June 17th, 2015 (Wide) (Indonesia)
June 18th, 2015 (Wide) (Australia)
June 18th, 2015 (Wide) (Malaysia)
June 18th, 2015 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
June 25th, 2015 (Wide), released as The Minions (New Zealand)
Video Release: November 24th, 2015 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for action and rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2367, 4/1/2015)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Franchise: Despicable Me, Minions
Cast, crew, or production detail: Directing Yourself, Intertitle, Prequel, Same Actor, Multiple Roles, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Antarctica, Florida, London, New York City, Orlando, FL
Plot point: Cavemen, Dinosaurs, Inventor, Origin Story, Pirates, Royalty, Super Villains, Vampire
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Time period setting: 1960s, Ancient Egypt, Prehistoric Times
Source:Spin-Off
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Illumination Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Starting as single-celled yellow organisms, Minions evolve through the ages, perpetually serving the most despicable of masters. Continuously unsuccessful at keeping these masters-from T. rex to Napoleon-the Minions find themselves without someone to serve and fall into a deep depression. But one Minion named Kevin has a plan, and he-alongside teenage rebel Stuart and lovable little Bob-ventures out into the world to find a new evil boss for his brethren to follow. The trio embarks upon a thrilling journey that ultimately leads them to their next potential master, Scarlet Overkill, the world's first-ever female super-villain. They travel from frigid Antarctica to 1960s New York City, ending in mod London, where they must face their biggest challenge to date: saving all of Minionkind...from annihilation.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$115,718,405 (34.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.90 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$74,000,000 (worldwide box office is 15.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,301 opening theaters/4,311 max. theaters, 7.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $476,762,449

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 10th, 2015 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: June 17th, 2015 (Wide) (Indonesia)
June 18th, 2015 (Wide) (Australia)
June 18th, 2015 (Wide) (Malaysia)
June 18th, 2015 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
June 25th, 2015 (Wide), released as The Minions (New Zealand)
Video Release: November 24th, 2015 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for action and rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2367, 4/1/2015)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Franchise: Despicable Me, Minions
Cast, crew, or production detail: Directing Yourself, Intertitle, Prequel, Same Actor, Multiple Roles, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Antarctica, Florida, London, New York City, Orlando, FL
Plot point: Cavemen, Dinosaurs, Inventor, Origin Story, Pirates, Royalty, Super Villains, Vampire
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Time period setting: 1960s, Ancient Egypt, Prehistoric Times
Source:Spin-Off
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Illumination Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
